The '''MorningStar-A''' was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] popular with [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] around the time of the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]].

==Characteristics==
They were armed with a large number of [[Missile/Legends|missiles]] that could prove deadly for starfighters and [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] alike.

==History==
A short time prior to the Invasion of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], a large number of MorningStars attacked the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] convoy led by [[Reeve Dallows]], [[Captain/Legends|captain]] of the freighter ''[[Charm's Way]]''. The attack was driven off with the aid of [[Bravo Squadron/Legends|Bravo Flight]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Essara Till/Legends|Essara Till]].

Later, MorningStars were among the mercenary retinue employed by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] to assassinate [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Amidala]]. However, this attack was driven off by Reeve Dallow's son, [[Rhys Dallows|Rhys]]. Upon returning to the Trade Federation, they were given orders to terminate [[Vana Sage]]'s contact with the Federation "by force".

==Variants==
There were at least two known variants of the MorningStar-A: the [[MorningStar-B starfighter|MorningStar-B]] and [[MorningStar-C starfighter|MorningStar-C]].

The B model was more of a dogfighting type, like the A model. However, the B model possessed more firepower than the A model and could also take more damage. The C model was an assault starfighter for use against capital ships. This variant naturally had the most firepower.
It was also more armored than the other types.

==Appearances==
*[[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']] {{1st}}
*''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]'' {{C|Multiplayer only}}
